metadata.title: Design a Personal Workflow Canvas with Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The self-auditing retrospective flag is what I want to internalize — that the canvas learns the human gate was too vague. Let me build workflow_canvas.py for my own weekly newsletter workstream and run it through three simulated uses. Show me: (1) the ten fields filled for the newsletter, with the save refused when I leave human_gate blank, (2) the markdown artifact it writes in consistent field order, and (3) the improvement note the retrospective appends after three runs, naming one structural fix to the allowed-inputs or human-gate field.

props.command: The self-auditing retrospective flag is what I want to internalize — that the canvas learns the human gate was too vague. Let me build workflow_canvas.py for my own weekly newsletter workstream and run it through three simulated uses. Show me: (1) the ten fields filled for the newsletter, with the save refused when I leave human_gate blank, (2) the markdown artifact it writes in consistent field order, and (3) the improvement note the retrospective appends after three runs, naming one structural fix to the allowed-inputs or human-gate field.
props.segment: Design a Personal Workflow Canvas with

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

Fifteen tasks, four buckets, two surprises — the workflow canvas reveals which Claude surface you should have been using all along. Building a system around Claude is not about more automation — it is about making your delegation decisions repeatable and auditable.

shot.notes: hook card: 'Fifteen tasks. Four buckets. Two surprises.' — CRIMSON on 'wrong surface', teal on 'repeatable and auditable'

beats.narration_text: Build workflow_canvas.py: an interactive CLI that prompts for ten fields — workstream, surface, allowed inputs, forbidden inputs, output artifact, constraints, human gate, memory scope, failure signal, improvement note. Refuse to save if human_gate is blank.

props.command:
claude "Write workflow_canvas.py:
  interactive CLI prompts for 10 fields:
    workstream, surface, allowed_inputs,
    forbidden_inputs, output_artifact,
    constraints, human_gate, memory_scope,
    failure_signal, improvement_note
  validate: forbidden_inputs and human_gate
  must be non-empty
  write markdown: workflow-[name].md
  REFUSE to save if human_gate is blank"

beats.narration_text: Check the code. The human gate field is validated before write — if it is blank, the save is refused with an error message. The output markdown matches the canvas template from chapter nine. The ten fields appear in a consistent order every time.

Run it with the book's client-briefing example. Ten fields filled interactively. The markdown file writes: workstream, surface Claude Cowork, allowed inputs briefing notes folder, human gate — reviewer must approve before send. The canvas is now a repeatable artifact.

shot.notes: terminal: interactive prompts filling in, workflow-client-briefing.md output shown — teal on 'human_gate' field, ten fields visible

beats.narration_text: Add a retrospective flag. Load an existing workflow file and prompt for improvement notes after three runs — the canvas learns from use.

props.command:
claude "update workflow_canvas.py:
  add --retrospective flag
  loads existing workflow-[name].md
  prompts for improvement_note
  after 3 runs on that workflow
  appends retrospective section to markdown"

beats.narration_text: Run the retrospective on the client-briefing canvas after three uses. The improvement note appends: the allowed-inputs field was too broad, the human gate needed a specific reviewer named. Two rounds of use, one structural improvement. The canvas audits itself.

shot.notes: terminal: --retrospective run — improvement note appended to workflow-client-briefing.md — 'Retrospective' section visible at bottom of markdown

beats.narration_text: Building a system around Claude is not about more automation — it is about making your delegation decisions repeatable and auditable. The canvas forces both. Without it, every Claude session starts from scratch and leaves no record.

shot.notes: teardown card: 'Ad-hoc delegation → no record → no improvement' (CRIMSON) vs 'Canvas → repeatable → auditable → improves with use' (teal)

beats.narration_text: Your move: run workflow_canvas.py for your highest-frequency Claude use case. Next reel: the process audit — a polished output is not evidence of a trustworthy workflow.

shot.notes: next-reel teaser: 'Next: Process Audit Bars' — segment 09 of the CLAUDE series

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Design a Personal Workflow Canvas with Claude] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
